hypothesis: Fine-tuning the transformer model on ATR-IR spectra of pure compounds—without requiring any labeled mixture ATR-IR data—will close the performance gap between gas-phase-trained and ATR-IR-evaluated models by more than 15 percentage points in Top-10 accuracy, because the dominant source of cross-instrument error resides in systematic baseline and refractive-index distortions that are equally present in pure-compound and mixture ATR-IR spectra and can therefore be corrected by domain adaptation on readily available single-component measurements.
